matching_rule: One base case matches when a promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample. Three additional case families match when the whitelist mean beta, the gene-name-related mean beta, or the all-numeric mean beta resolves to low, medium, or high methylation. Variant prediction rules also receive the actual sample REF -> ALT change; curated allele-change rules take precedence when the observed ALT allele or exact change has a known direction.
disclaimer: Predictive theses in this database are literature-guided research summaries derived from the bundled gene interpretation bundle. They are designed for exploratory synthesis in the UI and should not be treated as diagnostic or therapeutic claims.
concrete_variant_prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests an IGF1 receptor signaling-set-point thesis: the individual may carry a low-penetrance modifier of growth, endocrine, aging, pregnancy-history, cardiovascular, or cancer-cohort biology because IGF1R tunes PI3K-AKT and MAPK survival signaling. The prediction is pathway-contextual rather than a direct disease call.

variant_prediction_rules.prediction: Observed rs2229765 suggests this sample carries the IGF1R prediction context described by the gene-level thesis. The variant observed in this sample suggests an IGF1 receptor signaling-set-point thesis: the individual may carry a low-penetrance modifier of growth, endocrine, aging, pregnancy-history, cardiovascular, or cancer-cohort biology because IGF1R tunes PI3K-AKT and MAPK survival signaling. The prediction is pathway-contextual rather than a direct disease call. This specific marker is curated as G3174A / p.Glu1043Glu synonymous variant. rs2229765 is a common synonymous IGF1R polymorphism that has been studied as a signaling-set-point or haplotype marker in aging, pregnancy, endocrine, and cancer cohorts. The local database does not treat it as a pathogenic diagnostic variant; instead it is curated as a research association marker with context-dependent effects.
variant_prediction_rules.sample_change_template: This sample matched rs2229765 with the actual DNA change {change}. Anchor the IGF1R prediction to that observed REF -> ALT result instead of treating the variant as only a theoretical marker. The current preview does not infer zygosity or allele dose, so dosage-specific conclusions require genotype fields.

allele_change_rules.prediction: This sample row reports A as the observed alternate allele at IGF1R rs2229765. In the bundled studies, A-containing states were linked to male longevity and lower free IGF-1 in older AA men, and GA/AA was associated with reduced spontaneous-preterm-birth risk in one cohort. The resulting thesis is an IGF1R signaling-set-point modifier, not a deterministic endocrine or pregnancy prediction.
allele_change_rules.basis: Observed ALT A at rs2229765, the allele highlighted in local longevity and pregnancy-outcome association evidence.

evidence.label: PubMed 19460140 / PMCID PMC2692840: rs2229765 and male longevity
PubMed 28213921: rs2229765 and spontaneous preterm birth risk
PubMed 34527927: rs2229765 distribution in inflammatory arthritides and controls

allele_change_rules.prediction: This sample row reports G as the observed alternate allele at IGF1R rs2229765. G is the contrast state to the A-linked findings in the bundled longevity and pregnancy studies, so the sample should not be described as carrying the A-direction signal from those papers on this row. Keep the prediction at IGF1R pathway-context level unless genotype dosage is available.
allele_change_rules.basis: Observed ALT G at rs2229765, interpreted as the contrast state to the A-linked cohort findings.

evidence.label: PubMed 19460140 / PMCID PMC2692840: rs2229765 and male longevity
PubMed 28213921: rs2229765 and spontaneous preterm birth risk
PubMed 34527927: rs2229765 distribution in inflammatory arthritides and controls

variant_prediction_rules.basis: Synonymous coding change usually interpreted as a marker of subtle regulatory or linkage effects rather than a protein-altering lesion.

evidence.label: PubMed 19460140 / PMCID PMC2692840: rs2229765 and male longevity
PubMed 28213921: rs2229765 and spontaneous preterm birth risk

variant_prediction_rules.prediction: Observed rs2016347 suggests this sample carries the IGF1R prediction context described by the gene-level thesis. The variant observed in this sample suggests an IGF1 receptor signaling-set-point thesis: the individual may carry a low-penetrance modifier of growth, endocrine, aging, pregnancy-history, cardiovascular, or cancer-cohort biology because IGF1R tunes PI3K-AKT and MAPK survival signaling. The prediction is pathway-contextual rather than a direct disease call. This specific marker is curated as functional 3' regulatory IGF1R SNP. rs2016347 is curated here as a functional common IGF1R regulatory polymorphism that has been associated with altered breast involution, breast-cancer risk modification after hypertensive pregnancy, and selected cardiovascular or cancer outcomes in women with specific reproductive histories. The local database does not seed it as a pathogenic clinical allele.
variant_prediction_rules.sample_change_template: This sample matched rs2016347 with the actual DNA change {change}. Anchor the IGF1R prediction to that observed REF -> ALT result instead of treating the variant as only a theoretical marker. The current preview does not infer zygosity or allele dose, so dosage-specific conclusions require genotype fields.

allele_change_rules.prediction: This sample row reports T as the observed alternate allele at IGF1R rs2016347. The bundled reproductive-history studies associate T-carrier states with reduced terminal duct lobular unit counts and lower hormone-receptor-positive breast-cancer risk in specific hypertensive-pregnancy or preeclampsia contexts. The prediction is a context-dependent IGF-axis regulatory thesis, not an unconditional cancer-risk call.
allele_change_rules.basis: Observed ALT T at rs2016347, the allele highlighted in local reproductive-history and breast-involution evidence.

evidence.label: PubMed 31687025 / PMCID PMC6800903: rs2016347 and mammary gland involution
PubMed 33168159: rs2016347, cancer, and cardiovascular risk after hypertensive pregnancy
PubMed 36331687: rs2016347 and later-life breast cancer risk after preeclampsia
PubMed 35362533: rs2016347 and cardiovascular risk in endometriosis or PCOS cohorts

allele_change_rules.prediction: This sample row reports G as the observed alternate allele at IGF1R rs2016347. G is the contrast state to the T-carrier direction emphasized in the bundled breast-involution and hypertensive-pregnancy literature. Interpret this as IGF1R regulatory background rather than applying the T-carrier protective-context thesis.
allele_change_rules.basis: Observed ALT G at rs2016347, interpreted as the contrast state to the T-carrier cohort findings.

methylation_sources.label: Whitelist mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses the curated whitelist probes stored in the interpretation database for the current gene.

methylation_sources.label: Gene-name-related mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses rows whose gene annotation explicitly names the current gene.

methylation_sources.label: All numeric-row mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses every numeric beta value that survived preprocessing for the current sample.

cases.label: Gene variant found
cases.prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests an IGF1 receptor signaling-set-point thesis: the individual may carry a low-penetrance modifier of growth, endocrine, aging, pregnancy-history, cardiovascular, or cancer-cohort biology because IGF1R tunes PI3K-AKT and MAPK survival signaling. The prediction is pathway-contextual rather than a direct disease call. An observed IGF1R variant places this sample in the curated IGF1R research context. The bundled IGF1R records are intended as research and pathway-context markers, not as a diagnostic-grade interpretation set. Pathogenic IGF1R coding variants do exist in growth-disorder genetics, but the initial local database added here focuses on commonly studied polymorphisms and regulatory context relevant to the current SNP-and-methylation workflow. Commonly discussed IGF1R SNPs in this local database are best read as pathway modifiers, cohort markers, or regulatory correlates rather than as high-penetrance pathogenic variants.
cases.rationale: This is the base thesis that activates as soon as any promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample.
research_focus: IGF1R is heavily studied in cancer biology because altered IGF1 signaling can support proliferation, survival, and therapy resistance.
Human cohort work has also linked common IGF1R polymorphisms to aging-related phenotypes, hypertensive-pregnancy follow-up outcomes, cardiovascular traits, and selected birth or growth phenotypes.
The strongest methylation evidence in the local database is regulatory and biomarker-oriented rather than diagnostic on its own.

cases.label: Gene variant found + high whitelist mean beta
cases.prediction: When a IGF1R variant is paired with high methylation in the whitelist mean beta, the sample best fits a combined regulatory-context thesis. This pattern leans toward stronger regulatory restraint or reduced local accessibility around the locus. The variant side of the thesis is: The variant observed in this sample suggests an IGF1 receptor signaling-set-point thesis: the individual may carry a low-penetrance modifier of growth, endocrine, aging, pregnancy-history, cardiovascular, or cancer-cohort biology because IGF1R tunes PI3K-AKT and MAPK survival signaling. The prediction is pathway-contextual rather than a direct disease call. IGF1R methylation is best interpreted as regulatory context that may reflect local promoter accessibility or broader IGF-axis state rather than as a stand-alone disease call. The bundled EPIC subset includes promoter-adjacent CpG-island probes and many 5'UTR or gene-body probes, so this workbench should be read as a locus-level methylation view rather than a one-to-one proxy for every published IGF1R epigenetic assay. The bundled IGF1R records are intended as research and pathway-context markers, not as a diagnostic-grade interpretation set. Commonly discussed IGF1R SNPs in this local database are best read as pathway modifiers, cohort markers, or regulatory correlates rather than as high-penetrance pathogenic variants.
